steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researcher has no access to inorganic soil additives, so the correct amendment must be organic only. The data shows plant weight and root length for each treatment; the best choice produces the largest gains in both measures.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'biochar and NPK fertilizer': NPK fertilizer is inorganic — excluded by the constraint. ✗
  • Choice B — 'biochar and compost': both organic; produces 979.3 g weight (153.4 g above the 825.9 g control) and 6.76 cm root length (0.62 cm above control) — the largest gains of any organic option. ✓
  • Choice C — 'biochar alone': organic, but weight gain is only 89.8 g above control — far less than choice B. ✗
  • Choice D — 'compost alone': organic, but weight gain is only 38.9 g above control — the smallest improvement among organic choices. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
